_meta.purpose: WNS configuration: cascade trigger threshold + distance-decay budgets per NL layer.
_meta.pattern: Cascade-by-N. Each layer's counter is keyed by that layer's address tier (locality for NL2, district for NL3, ...). N events at one address fires that layer; the cascade then advances the parent counter at the layer above. Geometric — N=3 means ~3^5=243 leaf events per NL7 fire.
_meta.tunable: Threshold playtest-tuned. Adjust the JSON and restart — no code changes.
_meta.schema_change_2026_04_26: v1.0 had per-layer N thresholds (5/5/8/12/15/20) with the legacy NLTriggerManager. v2.0 replaces that with a single cascade threshold consumed by CascadeTriggerManager. The legacy keys under triggers.nl* are kept for backwards-read compatibility but ignored by the cascade path — the WMS→WNS bridge is the authoritative trigger source.

cascade._note: Single uniform threshold for the cascade trigger. CascadeTriggerManager increments per (layer, address) and fires when the count hits this value, then cascades the parent counter.
cascade.rationale: Three is the design default. Geometric progression (3^L for layer L above leaf) gives upper layers natural rarity without per-layer tuning. NL2 fires every 3 WMS interpretations at a locality; NL7 fires once per ~243 leaf events accumulated under one world. Designer can lower to 2 (more chatty) or raise to 5 (more rationed).

wms_context._note: WMS interpretation slice rendered into ${wms_context} per fire. Char budget enforces the soft <40% rule (WMS contributes <40% of WNS prompt context).

triggers._note: Legacy v1.0 per-layer N thresholds. Still consumed by NLTriggerManager (used by the legacy maybe_weave() path). The new cascade-by-N path (the WMS→WNS bridge) ignores these and uses cascade.threshold instead. Both can coexist; the cascade is the authoritative source for production.

distance_filter._note: Shallow-going-outward depth rules. Each firing tier has a list of layers it sees in 'full detail' (verbatim narrative) and 'brief summary' (truncated). char_caps controls truncation per depth.

char_caps._note: Char-budget per row at each depth. The cascading_context module's FADING_NARRATIVE_CHAR_CAP (240) is the canonical fading cap; these are soft hints distance_filter can use to trim candidate pools.

weaver_caps._note: Output caps the runtime enforces post-LLM. Per-fragment thread cap mirrors the per-layer prompt guidance.

call_wes._note: Calibration for the call_wes self-flag (now realized as inline <WES> XML tags). The LLM emits the tags; the runtime parses + dispatches. Optional deterministic fallbacks (NOT YET WIRED, hooks documented for future).

thread_count_threshold._note: If active threads at the firing address exceed N, force-fire WES even without explicit <WES> tag.

severity_threshold._note: If WMS severity at the firing layer crosses moderate→significant, force-fire.

arc_stage_transition._note: If a thread transitions rising_action -> falling_action at this firing, force-fire (turning point WES).
